title: 1901 Nobel Prize in Physics
body: The 1901 Nobel Prize in Physics was presented in a ceremony in Stockholm, Sweden to the German Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen, "in recognition of the extraordinary services he has rendered by the discovery of the remarkable rays subsequently named after him", namely, X-rays. It was the first of its kind resulting from the will of the Swedish chemist Alfred Nobel in 1895 to recognize "the person who made the most important discovery or invention in the field of physics".
